L. 117–347, title III, § 322136 Stat. 6206Jan. 5, 2023“(a).—In GeneralNot later than 1 year after the date on which the National Academy of Sciences submits the report required under section 3(c) of the Better Cybercrime Metrics Act [] ( note), and once each year thereafter, the Director of the Office for Victims of Crime shall submit a report to the Committee on the Judiciary of the Senate and the Committee on the Judiciary of the House of Representatives that addresses, to the extent data are available, the nature, extent, and amount of funding under the Victims of Crime Act of 1984 ( et seq.) for victims of cybercrimes against individuals.Pub. Transfer of Certain Unobligated Funds, , , provided that:Pub. L. 105–119, title I, § 109(b)111 Stat. 2457Nov. 26, 1997“Any unobligated sums hitherto available to the judicial branch pursuant to the paragraph repealed by subsection (a) [former ] shall be deemed to be deposits into the Crime Victims Fund as of the effective date hereof [] and may be used by the Director of the Office for Victims of Crime to improve services for the benefit of crime victims, including the processing and tracking of criminal monetary penalties and related litigation activities, in the Federal criminal justice system.”34 U.S.C. 20101(d)(1)Nov. 26, 1997

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: There is created in the Treasury a separate account to be known as the Crime Victims Fund (hereinafter in this subchapter referred to as the “Fund”).

children.heading: Fines deposited in Fund; penalties; forfeited appearance bonds
children.label: b Fines deposited in Fund; penalties; forfeited appearance bonds
i
children.text: section 11(d) of the Endangered Species Act (); and16 U.S.C. 1540(d)

children.label: ii
children.text: section 6(d) of the Lacey Act Amendments of 1981 (); and16 U.S.C. 3375(d)

children.label: i
children.text: the railroad unemployment insurance account pursuant to the Railroad Unemployment Insurance Act ( et seq.);45 U.S.C. 351

children.label: ii
children.text: the Postal Service Fund pursuant to sections 2601(a)(2) and 2003 of title 39 and for the purposes set forth in ;section 404(a)(7) of title 39

children.label: iii
children.text: the navigable waters revolving fund pursuant to section 311 of the Federal Water Pollution Control Act (); and33 U.S.C. 1321

children.label: iv
children.text: county public school funds pursuant to ;section 3613 of title 18
penalty assessments collected under ;section 3013 of title 181See References in Text note below.1
the proceeds of forfeited appearance bonds, bail bonds, and collateral collected under ;section 3146 of title 18
any money ordered to be paid into the Fund under ;section 3671(c)(2) of title 18

children.label: A
children.text: attaches conditions inconsistent with applicable laws or regulations; or

children.label: B
children.text: is conditioned upon or would require the expenditure of appropriated funds that are not available to the Office for Victims of Crime; and

children.label: A
children.text: a deferred prosecution agreement; or

children.label: B
children.text: a non-prosecution agreement.

children.heading: Retention of sums in Fund; availability for expenditure without fiscal year limitation
children.label: c Retention of sums in Fund; availability for expenditure without fiscal year limitation
children.text: Sums deposited in the Fund shall remain in the Fund and be available for expenditure under this subchapter for grants under this subchapter without fiscal year limitation. Notwithstanding subsection (d)(5), all sums deposited in the Fund in any fiscal year that are not made available for obligation by Congress in the subsequent fiscal year shall remain in the Fund for obligation in future fiscal years, without fiscal year limitation.

children.heading: Availability for judicial branch administrative costs; grant program percentages
children.label: d Availability for judicial branch administrative costs; grant program percentages
A
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the first $10,000,000 deposited in the Fund shall be available for grants under .section 20104 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: For any fiscal year for which the amount deposited in the Fund is greater than the amount deposited in the Fund for fiscal year 1998, the $10,000,000 referred to in subparagraph (A) plus an amount equal to 50 percent of the increase in the amount from fiscal year 1998 shall be available for grants under .section 20104 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: Amounts available under this subparagraph for any fiscal year shall not exceed $20,000,000.

children.label: i
children.text: the United States Attorneys Offices and the Federal Bureau of Investigation to provide and improve services for the benefit of crime victims in the Federal criminal justice system (as described in section 3771 or section 3772, as it relates to direct services, of title 18 and ) through victim coordinators, victims’ specialists, and advocates, including for the administrative support of victim coordinators and advocates providing such services; andsection 20141 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: a Victim Notification System.

children.label: B
children.text: Amounts made available under subparagraph (A) may not be used for any purpose that is not specified in clause (i) or (ii) of subparagraph (A).

children.label: A
children.text: 47.5 percent shall be available for grants under ;section 20102 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: 47.5 percent shall be available for grants under ; andsection 20103(a) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: 5 percent shall be available for grants under .section 20103(c) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: In addition to the amounts distributed under paragraphs (2), (3), and (4), the Director may set aside up to $50,000,000 from the amounts transferred to the Fund in response to the airplane hijackings and terrorist acts that occurred on , as an antiterrorism emergency reserve. The Director may replenish any amounts obligated from such reserve in subsequent fiscal years by setting aside up to 5 percent of the amounts remaining in the Fund in any fiscal year after distributing amounts under paragraphs (2), (3) and (4). Such reserve shall not exceed $50,000,000.September 11, 2001

children.label: B
children.text: The antiterrorism emergency reserve referred to in subparagraph (A) may be used for supplemental grants under and to provide compensation to victims of international terrorism under .section 20105 of this titlesection 20106 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: Amounts in the antiterrorism emergency reserve established pursuant to subparagraph (A) may be carried over from fiscal year to fiscal year. Notwithstanding subsection (c) and section 619 of the Departments of Commerce, Justice, and State, the Judiciary, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2001 (and any similar limitation on Fund obligations in any future Act, unless the same should expressly refer to this section), any such amounts carried over shall not be subject to any limitation on obligations from amounts deposited to or available in the Fund.

children.label: A
children.text: The Director may set aside up to $10,000,000 of the amounts remaining in the Fund in any fiscal year after distributing the amounts under paragraphs (2), (3), and (4), in a Child Pornography Victims Reserve, which may be used by the Attorney General for payments under .section 2259(d) of title 18

children.label: B
children.text: Amounts in the reserve may be carried over from fiscal year to fiscal year, but the total amount of the reserve shall not exceed $10,000,000. Notwithstanding subsection (c) and any limitation on Fund obligations in any future Act, unless the same should expressly refer to this section, any such amounts carried over shall not be subject to any limitation on obligations from amounts deposited to or available in the Fund.

children.heading: Amounts awarded and unspent
children.label: e Amounts awarded and unspent
children.text: Any amount awarded as part of a grant under this subchapter that remains unspent at the end of a fiscal year in which the grant is made may be expended for the purpose for which the grant is made at any time during the 3 succeeding fiscal years, at the end of which period, any remaining unobligated sums shall be available for deposit into the emergency reserve fund referred to in subsection (d)(5) at the discretion of the Director, except that renewals and extensions beyond that period may be granted at the discretion of the Attorney General. Any remaining unobligated sums shall be returned to the Fund.

children.heading: “Offenses against the United States” as excluding
children.label: f “Offenses against the United States” as excluding

children.text: a criminal violation of the Uniform Code of Military Justice ( et seq.);10 U.S.C. 801
an offense against the laws of the District of Columbia; and
an offense triable by an Indian tribal court or Court of Indian Offenses.

children.heading: Grants for Indian tribes; child abuse cases
children.label: g Grants for Indian tribes; child abuse cases
A
children.text: the handling of child abuse cases, particularly cases of child sexual abuse, in a manner which limits additional trauma to the child victim; and

children.label: B
children.text: the investigation and prosecution of cases of child abuse, particularly child sexual abuse.

The Attorney General may use 5 percent of the funds available under subsection (d)(2) (prior to distribution) for grants to Indian tribes to establish child victim assistance programs, as appropriate.
As used in this subsection, the term “tribe” has the meaning given that term in section 5304(b) of title 25.2So in original. Probably should be “ ‘Indian tribe’ ”.21
